fileNamingConvention.title: File Naming Convention
fileNamingConvention.content:
Each submission file must be named using the format: StudentName_StudentID.ext
Example: Jane-Smith_12345678.docx or Jane-Smith_12345678.pptx
The AI uses the filename to identify each student in the grades report.

supportedFormats.title: Supported File Formats

formats.notes: Standard student essay or report. Text is extracted automatically.
Slide text and notes are extracted and sent to the AI for marking.

supportedFormats.matrixNote: The marking matrix must be a .docx Word document. Download the demo template to get started.

markingMatrix.title: Marking Matrix
markingMatrix.importance: The marking matrix is crucial for determining how well the AI evaluates submissions. Good results depend on having a clear, detailed marking scheme. Here's an example format:

grades.task1: Incomplete or incoherent design. Major errors in topology, addressing, or configuration. Minimal/no evidence of understanding protocols (RIP, DHCP, VLAN).
grades.task2: Minimal or no testing. Results missing, incorrect, or not explained. No verification of connectivity between sites.
grades.task3: Unstructured or incomplete report. Missing major sections. Weak/no referencing. Poor academic style.

grades.task1: Basic design attempted but with errors in topology or addressing. Limited configuration details. Partial application of required protocols.
grades.task2: At least two tests attempted but limited in scope. Partial connectivity shown. Weak explanation of results.
grades.task3: Report follows template loosely but lacks clarity. Some sections missing or superficial. Referencing inconsistent.

grades.task1: Clear design covering core devices and addressing. Some minor errors or omissions in configuration. Evidence of protocol implementation but not fully tested/consistent.
grades.task2: Two tests provided including routers/PCs in different cities. Basic explanation of results. Some errors/omissions in verification of RIP/DHCP/VLAN.
grades.task3: Clear structure following template. Adequate detail in most sections (design, tests, evaluation). Some evidence of analysis. References included but limited.

grades.task1: Well-structured design with correct addressing scheme (CIDR), secure device configs (SSH/passwords), and correct use of RIP/DHCP/VLAN. Some areas could be more detailed or optimised.
grades.task2: Multiple tests conducted with screenshots/output. Connectivity across sites demonstrated. Correct verification of at least RIP/DHCP/VLAN. Results explained with some discussion.
grades.task3: Well-written and structured report. Clear explanation of design/testing with supporting figures/screenshots. References correctly used. Some critical reflection included.

grades.task1: Comprehensive design with accurate configurations across devices. Efficient addressing plan, evidence of security best practices, and well-documented protocol implementation.
grades.task2: Extensive, well-structured testing showing thorough coverage. Correct use of ping/traceroute/CLI outputs. All required protocols verified. Clear interpretation of test results.
grades.task3: Professionally presented report. Strong integration of design/testing evidence. Critical discussion and evaluation present. Good academic style and referencing.

grades.task1: Professional-level design with excellent clarity and justification. Fully optimised addressing, secure and scalable configuration. Integration of advanced Packet Tracer features or creative enhancements beyond brief.
grades.task2: Professional test strategy with comprehensive evidence (screenshots, logs, analysis). Critical evaluation of test outcomes. Use of advanced verification beyond brief.
grades.task3: High-quality report. Excellent clarity, justification, and critical evaluation. Strong visuals/diagrams, flawless academic writing, and comprehensive referencing.

help.futureNote: Note: More file formats will be supported in the future with better integration.
